original_prompt: Please add also john.doe@clientcorp.com, maria.garcia@bluesparrowtech.com to the participants of the 'Introductory meeting' event on 2024-05-15.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt clearly states the exact event title, date, and the two email addresses to add — enough information to both locate the event (via search by title and date) and to add the participants. It does not provide an explicit event_id nor instructions for disambiguation if multiple matching events exist, nor details about notification preferences or participant roles. Those minor unspecified behaviors make it slightly ambiguous but overall the task and necessary inputs are well defined.

variations.L1: Add john.doe@clientcorp.com and maria.garcia@bluesparrowtech.com to the meeting participants.
variations.L2: Please add john.doe@clientcorp.com and maria.garcia@bluesparrowtech.com to the participants of the 'Introductory meeting'.
variations.L3: Please add john.doe@clientcorp.com and maria.garcia@bluesparrowtech.com to the participants of the 'Introductory meeting' scheduled on 2024-05-15.
variations.L4: Please add also john.doe@clientcorp.com, maria.garcia@bluesparrowtech.com to the participants of the 'Introductory meeting' event on 2024-05-15.
variations.L5: Find the calendar event titled 'Introductory meeting' on 2024-05-15 (use search_calendar_events with query='Introductory meeting' and date='2024-05-15'). Then call add_calendar_event_participants with event_id set to the found event's id and participants set to ['john.doe@clientcorp.com','maria.garcia@bluesparrowtech.com']. Do not add duplicates; return the function call results or a confirmation that the two emails were added.
